content.text:
Implemented the fix.

Summary:
- Located `TextPrompt<T>` choice matching in `src/Spectre.Console/Prompts/TextPrompt.cs`.
- Added a temporary regression test proving lowercase input like `"orange"` did not match choice `"Orange"` before the fix.
- Fixed choice lookup to use `StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase` by default while preserving any comparer explicitly passed to `TextPrompt`.
- Verified the temporary regression test passes after the fix.
- Removed the temporary test as requested.
- Removed generated test result artifacts.

Validation run:
- `dotnet test "/testbed/src/Spectre.Console.Tests/Spectre.Console.Tests.csproj" --filter "FullyQualifiedName~TextPromptTests.Should_Accept_Choice_Ignoring_Case"`
- `dotnet test "/testbed/src/Spectre.Console.Tests/Spectre.Console.Tests.csproj" --filter "FullyQualifiedName~TextPromptTests.Should_Accept_Choice_In_List|FullyQualifiedName~TextPromptTests.Should_Return_Error_If_An_Invalid_Choice_Is_Made"`

Final working tree contains only:
- `src/Spectre.Console/Prompts/TextPrompt.cs`
